description: Add Google Cloud Vision API as an OCR engine with native PDF support

userStories.title: Add google-cloud-vision dependency and create Google OCR engine for images
userStories.description: Add the google-cloud-vision Python SDK as a dependency and implement a new engine module at engines/google.py that performs text detection on image files using the Vision API. The engine should support API key authentication (checking GOOGLE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY in order) and fall back to Application Default Credentials. It should validate the input file and return extracted text.
acceptanceCriteria: google-cloud-vision is listed in packages/ocr_tool/pyproject.toml dependencies
uv lock completes without errors after adding the dependency
File packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/engines/google.py exists and defines a function extract_text_google(file_path: Path, model: str | None = None) -> str
The function checks for API keys in this order: GOOGLE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY. If found, it creates ImageAnnotatorClient with client_options={'api_key': key}
If no API key is found, the function falls back to GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS or google.auth.default()
The function raises OcrError with code MISSING_CREDENTIALS only when no API key AND no ADC credentials are available
The function raises OcrError with code IMAGE_NOT_FOUND when the input file does not exist
The function uses google.cloud.vision.ImageAnnotatorClient and document_text_detection for image files

Unit tests exist at tests/test_engines/test_google.py covering: missing credentials, missing file, successful image extraction with API key (mocked), successful image extraction with ADC fallback (mocked), and empty result handling
userStories.notes: Use document_text_detection (not text_detection) as it provides better structured results. For API key auth, pass client_options={'api_key': key} to ImageAnnotatorClient(). The env var lookup order is: GOOGLE_API_KEY > GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY > GEMINI_API_KEY > ADC. This allows reusing existing Google API keys without needing a service account.

userStories.title: Integrate Google engine into models and service layer
userStories.description: Expand the OcrRequest mode literal to include 'google', update the service layer to dispatch to the new engine, and wire up the model_used field correctly for the google engine.
acceptanceCriteria: OcrRequest.mode in packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/models.py accepts Literal['local', 'llm', 'google']
OcrResult.mode in packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/models.py accepts Literal['local', 'llm', 'google']
packages/ocr_tool/src/ocr_tool/service.py imports extract_text_google and dispatches to it when mode is 'google'
run_ocr returns model_used='google-vision' (or similar constant) when mode is 'google' and no custom model is provided
Existing tests in tests/test_service.py still pass: cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run pytest tests/test_service.py -q exits 0
New test in tests/test_service.py verifies google mode dispatches to extract_text_google

userStories.title: Add PDF support for the Google engine
userStories.description: Extend the Google OCR engine to accept PDF files using the Vision API's batch_annotate_files with inline content. The service layer should detect PDF input by file extension and use the appropriate Vision API method. Raise a clear error if the PDF exceeds 5 pages.
acceptanceCriteria: extract_text_google in engines/google.py accepts .pdf files and uses batch_annotate_files with InputConfig(mime_type='application/pdf', content=<bytes>)
The function raises OcrError with code PDF_TOO_LARGE when the PDF exceeds 5 pages, with a message explaining the limit
The function raises OcrError with code INVALID_FILE when the file is neither a supported image format nor a PDF
OcrRequest.image_path field description is updated to mention PDF support (or field is renamed to input_path with backwards-compatible alias)
Unit tests in tests/test_engines/test_google.py cover: successful PDF extraction (mocked), PDF page limit error, and unsupported file type error
The default output_path for a PDF input is <stem>.txt (same logic as images)
userStories.notes: Use PyPDF2 or just send to the API and let it fail if too large. Prefer checking page count locally with a lightweight approach if possible. The 5-page limit is for synchronous inline processing via batch_annotate_files.

userStories.title: Update CLI to support google mode and PDF input
userStories.description: Update the CLI extract command to accept 'google' as a valid mode value and to accept PDF files as input. Update the mode validation and help text accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: Running ocr-tool extract --image test.png --mode google routes to the google engine (verified via mocked test)
The mode validation in cli.py accepts 'local', 'llm', and 'google' (not just 'local' and 'llm')
The --image option help text mentions PDF support when using google mode
Running ocr-tool extract --image test.pdf --mode google does not fail validation (PDF is accepted)
Running ocr-tool extract --image test.pdf --mode local emits a structured JSON error with code UNSUPPORTED_FILE_TYPE indicating local mode only supports images
Existing CLI tests still pass: cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run pytest tests/test_cli.py -q exits 0
New CLI tests cover: google mode success (mocked), PDF with non-google mode error
userStories.notes: The PDF-with-local-mode validation can live in service.py rather than cli.py — either is acceptable as long as the error is structured.

userStories.title: Full test suite passes with lint and type checks
userStories.description: Ensure the entire ocr_tool package passes all tests, ruff lint, ruff format check, and mypy type checking. Fix any issues introduced by the new engine.
acceptanceCriteria: cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run pytest -q exits 0 with all tests passing
cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run ruff check src/ tests/ exits 0
cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/ exits 0
cd packages/ocr_tool && uv run mypy src/ exits 0 (or exits 0 with only pre-existing google-cloud type stub warnings)
No test uses real network calls — all Google Vision API calls are mocked in tests
userStories.notes: google-cloud-vision has protobuf-generated types. If mypy complains about missing stubs, add google-cloud-vision stubs or use type: ignore on specific imports. This is acceptable.
